name: Naturepedia Bioelectric Plant Intelligence Knowledge Mesh
description: Paid relationship mesh connecting bioelectric systems, plant intelligence, plant electrophysiology, root intelligence, soil electrical networks, electrical ecology, mycorrhizal signaling, and living information fields.
architecture: Bioelectric Systems → Plant Electrophysiology → Plant Intelligence → Root Intelligence → Soil Electrical Networks → Mycorrhizal Networks → Electrical Ecology → Living Information Fields

relationshipMesh.relationship: Bioelectric systems connect to plants through membrane voltage, action potentials, wound signaling, growth regulation, and environmental sensing.
Electrical signaling supports sensing, response, adaptation, memory, defense, and coordination throughout the plant.
Roots integrate electrical, chemical, moisture, gravity, microbial, fungal, nutrient, and environmental signals.
Roots interact with electrically active soils, minerals, microbes, fungi, and moisture gradients.
Electrical pathways and fungal networks create underground communication routes for nutrients, warning signals, and resource allocation.
Plant-fungal systems exchange chemical cues, electrical gradients, defense signals, root exudates, and ecological information.
Plant communication becomes part of broader biological information systems spanning roots, fungi, microbes, soil, water, and ecosystems.
Distributed biological signaling creates landscape-scale information fields connecting organisms and ecosystems.
Electrical signaling contributes to ecosystem feedback, resilience, regeneration, and adaptive environmental responses.
